perturbation.original_text: 5. PAYMENT. 5.1 ADVERTISING FEES. After the Launch Date, i-Escrow shall pay 2TheMart advertising fees based on the number of Transaction Inquiries. This advertising fees shall consist of a per Transaction Inquiry amount calculated by multiplying 0.025% by the amount of the average Transaction from all Customers in the preceding quarter. The formula for arriving at the per Transaction Inquiry amount may be revised from time to time during the term of this Agreement to reflect present market conditions ("the Adjusted Rate"), but only by mutual consent of the parties after good faith discussions. The Adjusted Rate shall be added as an addendum to this Agreement. 5.2 REPORTING. Within two (2) weeks following the end of each calendar quarter, i-Escrow shall provide to 2TheMart a report, describing for each quarter: the number of new registrations through the Co-Branded Pages; the number of Transaction Inquiries from Customers; the total number of Transactions from such inquiries; the total dollar value of the Transactions.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. PAYMENT. 5.1 ADVERTISING FEES. After the Launch Date, i-Escrow shall pay 2TheMart advertising fees based on mutually agreed-upon terms. These terms will be outlined in subsequent documentation. 5.2 REPORTING. Within two (2) weeks following the end of each calendar quarter, i-Escrow shall provide to 2TheMart a report, describing for each quarter: the number of new registrations through the Co-Branded Pages; the number of Transaction Inquiries from Customers; the total number of Transactions from such inquiries; the total dollar value of the Transactions.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a formula for calculating advertising fees based on Transaction Inquiries. The modified version removes this formula and replaces it with a vague reference to 'mutually agreed-upon terms' and 'subsequent documentation' which never exists. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 5.2 requires reporting on metrics directly tied to the now-undefined fee calculation method. Without a clear formula, it's impossible to determine how advertising fees are calculated, making Section 5.2 reporting requirements practically unenforceable and internally contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.2 REPORTING. Within two (2) weeks following the end of each calendar quarter, i-Escrow shall provide to 2TheMart a report, describing for each quarter: the number of new registrations through the Co-Branded Pages; the number of Transaction Inquiries from Customers; the total number of Transactions from such inquiries; the total dollar value of the Transactions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific formula for calculating advertising fees, replacing it with a vague reference to 'mutually agreed-upon terms' and 'subsequent documentation.' This directly contradicts Section 5.2, which mandates reporting on metrics ('Transaction Inquiries', 'total dollar value of the Transactions') directly used in the now-removed fee calculation. Without the formula, the reporting requirements in Section 5.2 are rendered practically unenforceable and internally inconsistent, because i-Escrow cannot calculate advertising fees based on Transaction Inquiries anymore.

perturbation.original_text: 6. RIGHTS AND STANDARDS. Source: 2THEMART COM INC, 10-12G, 8/26/1999 6.1 CONTENT. 2TheMart hereby grants to i-Escrow a worldwide, non-exclusive right to use, reproduce, distribute, publicly perform, publicly display and digitally perform the 2TheMart Content soley with respect to and in conjunction with the Co-Branded Site all with the prior written consent of 2TheMart, for the term of this Agreement. i-Escrow hereby grants to 2TheMart a worldwide, non-exclusive right to use, reproduce, distribute, publicly perform, publicly display and digitally perform the i-Escrow Content on or in conjunction with 2TheMart auctions.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. RIGHTS AND STANDARDS. 6.1 CONTENT. 2TheMart hereby grants to i-Escrow a worldwide, right to use the 2TheMart Content soley with respect to and in conjunction with the Co-Branded Site for the term of this Agreement. i-Escrow hereby grants to 2TheMart a worldwide, non-exclusive right to use, reproduce, distribute, publicly perform, publicly display and digitally perform the i-Escrow Content on or in conjunction with 2TheMart auctions.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'non-exclusive', 'reproduce, distribute, publicly perform, publicly display and digitally perform' and 'all with the prior written consent of 2TheMart', from the first sentence, concerning rights granted by 2TheMart to i-Escrow, the modification implies that i-Escrow now has exclusive and unrestricted rights to use 2TheMart's content. This creates a contradiction because the second sentence still grants 2TheMart only non-exclusive rights to i-Escrow's content, suggesting unequal terms for content usage, even though 6.2 will be making reference to the original statement, creating ambiguity regarding content ownership and usage rights, making the statement contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2 CONTENT OWNERSHIP. Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, as between 2TheMart and i-Escrow: (a) 2TheMart and its suppliers retain all rights, title and interest in and to all intellectual property rights embodied in or associated with the 2TheMart Content, and b) i-Escrow and its suppliers retain all rights, title and interest in and to all intellectual property rights embodied in or associated with the i-Escrow Content and Co-Branded Site.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'non-exclusive', 'reproduce, distribute, publicly perform, publicly display and digitally perform' and 'all with the prior written consent of 2TheMart' from the rights granted by 2TheMart to i-Escrow. This alteration implies that i-Escrow now possesses exclusive and unrestricted rights to utilize 2TheMart's content, directly conflicting with section 6.2 CONTENT OWNERSHIP, which states that 2TheMart and its suppliers retain all rights, title and interest in and to all intellectual property rights embodied in or associated with the 2TheMart Content. The modified text effectively grants i-Escrow rights that contradict 2TheMart's ownership as defined elsewhere in the agreement, creating a significant in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8.5 EFFECTS OF TERMINATION. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement for any reason: (a) all rights granted herein shall terminate, (b) i-Escrow shall pay all amounts owed to 2TheMart within six (6) weeks of termination, and (c) each party shall remove the other party's content and Marks from their servers. Notwithstanding the foregoing, unless this Agreement was terminated for a material breach, all provisions of this Agreement shall survive to the extent necessary for i-Escrow to complete any Customer transactions which are pending at the time of expiration or termination. Sections 1, 7, 8.5 [EFFECTS OF TERMINATION], 9, 10, 11 and 12 shall survive expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.5 EFFECTS OF TERMINATION. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement for any reason: (a) all rights granted herein shall terminate, (b) i-Escrow shall pay all amounts owed to 2TheMart within six (6) weeks of termination, and (c) each party shall remove the other party's content and Marks from their servers. Notwithstanding the foregoing, all provisions of this Agreement shall survive to the extent necessary for i-Escrow to complete any Customer transactions which are pending at the time of expiration or termination. Sections 1, 7, 9, 10, 11 and 12 shall survive expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'unless this Agreement was terminated for a material breach' from the 'notwithstanding' clause, the modified text now states that all provisions necessary for i-Escrow to complete pending customer transactions survive termination regardless of the reason for termination (including material breach). By removing '8.5 [EFFECTS OF TERMINATION]' the modified text now contradicts itself. This creates an internal inconsistency because subsection (a) states that 'all rights granted herein shall terminate', but other rights remain active because of the survival of other clauses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.5 EFFECTS OF TERMINATION. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement for any reason: (a) all rights granted herein shall terminate, (b) i-Escrow shall pay all amounts owed to 2TheMart within six (6) weeks of termination, and (c) each party shall remove the other party's content and Marks from their servers. Notwithstanding the foregoing, all provisions of this Agreement shall survive to the extent necessary for i-Escrow to complete any Customer transactions which are pending at the time of expiration or termination. Sections 1, 7, 9, 10, 11 and 12 shall survive expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The removal of "unless this Agreement was terminated for a material breach" expands the survival clause to include situations where a material breach occurred. Additionally, removing '8.5 [EFFECTS OF TERMINATION]' from the list of surviving sections contradicts the provision that all rights granted herein terminate upon expiration or termination, as subsection 8.5(a) stating "all rights granted herein shall terminate" will also terminate, but the other survival clauses need the definition from Section 8.5 to function. Therefore, the changed text creates a direct conflict within the document, leading to an in-text contradiction.
